source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Black Elk Energy Offshore Operating LLC VR369A Fire Incident

context.operator: Black Elk Energy Offshore Operating LLC

actions_taken: Operator identified Burner Safety Low alarm at master panel
Operator isolated manual block valves to supply gas
Operator used Gaitronics paging system to alert personnel
Multiple operators applied handheld fire extinguisher bursts to extinguish fire
Operators monitored area post-extinguishment and assessed cause
event.summary: On April 18, 2014 at approximately 1900 hours, a fire occurred in the Heater Treater (NBK-2100) on the VR369A platform operated by Black Elk Energy Offshore Operating LLC in the Gulf of Mexico. The fire was caused by a pin hole that developed in the fire tube due to pit corrosion from frequent high-dosage batch treatments with acid-based de-emulsifier (Gyptron TA-21). Produced oil leaked through the pin hole into the fire tube and sprayed near the main burner, providing a steady fuel source. The operator responded to a Burner Safety Low alarm, isolated gas supply manually, activated the ESD station, and alerted personnel. The fire was successfully extinguished using handheld fire extinguishers. The incident resulted in $15,000 in property damage to the fire tube.
recommendations: Inspect the fire tube when possible hazards are first identified
Implement formal documentation of daily vessel monitoring (pressures, levels, temperatures)
Establish preventive maintenance and inspection schedule for fire tube given acid exposure
Review chemical program and heat management to prevent emulsion pad formation
Ensure adequate records of all maintenance and inspection activities

hazards.name: High-pressure heated oil and gas in heater treater
Ignition sources in heater treater fired component

threats.name: Pin hole develops in fire tube due to pit corrosion
Produced oil leaks from fire tube into burner area
Oil mist contacts ignition source (main burner)

controls.name: Fire tube inspection and maintenance program
controls.barrier_role: Detect and prevent fire tube degradation before failure
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to identify corrosion before pin hole develops

supporting_text: there were neither any records nor recollection within BEEOO to support if and/or when prior maintenance/inspection had been performed with regards to the fire tube
There was a lack of documentation onboard the facility showing that the fire tube had ever been removed from the vessel and inspected in the past

BEEOO in-house Root Cause Analysis (RCA) from December 30, 2013 had identified that due to large quantities of acid being pumped directly to the treater (100 - 150 gallons per week), inspection of the fire tube should be performed; however, this had not taken place prior to the incident

controls.name: Burner Safety Low (BSL) alarm system
controls.barrier_role: Detect low burner safety condition and shut off gas supply
controls.lod_basis: Automatic detection and alarm of unsafe burner conditions

supporting_text: witnesses heard a platform process alarm and an operator responded to the master panel to identify the cause
the operator identified a Burner Safety Low (BSL) alarm indication
the BSL is only required to shut-off gas supply to the heater treater fired component and the line heater burner

controls.name: Manual isolation of gas supply via block valves
controls.barrier_role: Manually shut off fuel gas supply to burner
controls.lod_basis: Manual intervention to isolate fuel source when alarm detected

supporting_text: he isolated the manual block valves associated to the supply gas and then realized the fire still had not subsided

the fire still had not subsided because produced oil leaked inside the fire tube and sprayed in the vicinity of the main burner which enabled a steady fuel source although the burner fuel gas had been shut off

controls.name: Emergency Shut-Down (ESD) station activation
controls.barrier_role: Shut down production and isolate all fuel sources
controls.lod_basis: Automated emergency shutdown of production systems

supporting_text: the operator utilized the Gaitronics paging system to alert other personnel on the platform of the situation and then activated a nearby Emergency Shut-Down (ESD) station
oil and gas production momentarily remained online until such time the operator manually shut-in production via the ESD

controls.name: Personnel communication and coordination
controls.barrier_role: Alert personnel to emergency and coordinate response
controls.lod_basis: Rapid communication to mobilize emergency response

supporting_text: the operator utilized the Gaitronics paging system to alert other personnel on the platform of the situation
the other operators arrived to the scene and together they opened the fire tube inspection plate and applied two short bursts from a handheld fire extinguisher

controls.name: Handheld fire extinguisher
controls.lod_basis: Direct fire suppression equipment for emergency response

supporting_text: the other operators arrived to the scene and together they opened the fire tube inspection plate and applied two short bursts from a handheld fire extinguisher which successfully extinguished the fire

controls.name: Chemical program review and acid dosage management
controls.barrier_role: Control acid exposure to prevent corrosion
controls.lod_basis: Manage chemical treatment to minimize corrosion risk

supporting_text: Batch treating with an acid based de-emulsifier was being carried out as needed, which was typically multiple times a week
Approximately 20 gallons of the acid based de-emulsifier (Gyptron TA-21) were being pumped at a rate of 1.33 gallons per minute
The chemical program and also a lack of sufficient heat were considered possible causes of the emulsion pad
due to large quantities of acid being pumped directly to the treater (100 - 150 gallons per week)

controls.name: Heater treater temperature control and monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Maintain adequate temperature to prevent emulsion pad formation
controls.lod_basis: Operational control to prevent conditions that accelerate corrosion

supporting_text: The Heater Treater electric grid was also out of service
The vessel temperature must be maintained below 130 degrees Fahrenheit in order to meet departing pipeline limits set by Shell Pipeline
Daily, the operators monitored pressures, levels, and temperatures associated with the vessel but the information was not formally documented
a lack of sufficient heat were considered possible causes of the emulsion pad

controls.name: Regulatory inspection and compliance oversight
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and enforce corrective actions
controls.lod_basis: External regulatory oversight to identify and mandate corrective actions

supporting_text: On April 9, 2014, prior to the incident, the BSEE Lake Charles District had begun conducting an inspection of the facility. During the inspection, inspectors developed concern regarding the integrity of the fire tube inside the Heater Treater NBK-2100
On April 10, 2014, inspectors returned to VR 369A to resume the inspection and continued gathering information associated with the integrity of the heater treater fire tube
While the inspection remained ongoing, prior to receiving any of the requested inspection documentation from BEEOO, the fire occurred on April 18, 2014

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
